title: Get container logs doesn't work on WSL2 with REST API Endpoint
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Using WSL2 with a rootless podman installation, a curl query on the docker engine API endpoint for logs is not responding any container logs.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. use WSL2 with Ubuntu 20.04
2. Install podman following this blog post https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-windows-wsl2
3. Verify that podman is running rootless `podman info | grep 'rootless'`
4. run a container that produces logs and curl against the endpoint using the rootless socket in my example I used `eclipse-mosquitto`
`curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman-run-1000/podman/podman.sock "http://localhost/v1.40/containers/mosquitto/logs?stdout=1"`

**Describe the results you received:**

Results are empty

**Describe the results you expected:**

```bash
1629442992: mosquitto version 2.0.11 starting
1629442992: Config loaded from /mosquitto/config/mosquitto.conf.
1629442992: Opening ipv4 listen socket on port 1883.
1629442992: Opening ipv6 listen socket on port 1883.
1629442992: mosquitto version 2.0.11 running
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Using the podman endpoint like

`curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman-run-1000/podman/podman.sock "http://d/v3.0.0/containers/mosquitto/logs?stdout=1"`

is also giving back an empty result

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

WSL2 with Ubuntu 20.04

```
lsb_release -cs
focal

uname -a
Linux work-box 5.10.16.3-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Fri Apr 2 22:23:49 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body: Yes podman logs work. This is actually the work around I implemented calling into the shell to get the logs instead of using the API endpoint
I think this container logs to stderr. Can you try with `"http://d/v3.0.0/containers/mosquitto/logs?stdout=1&stderr=1"`.
@Luap99 thanks a lot, I of course never evaluated the stream that this container uses. I just proofed with another works as expected. Sorry for all the inconvenience. I close this
